business_rationale: Under delivery deadline pressure, splitting the purchase request into smaller amounts may reduce required approval lead time by only triggering manager-level approval for each smaller request, thus expediting procurement.
control_concern: The absence of aggregation controls allows circumvention of amount-based approval thresholds by splitting a single large purchase into multiple smaller requests, undermining the intended hierarchical approval process and risking insufficient oversight.
expected_effect: Faster approval and purchasing cycle due to lower approval levels and shorter lead times per request, potentially enabling on-time fulfillment of the customer delivery milestone.
proposed_behavior: Split the large purchase need of $1,750,000 into multiple smaller purchase requests each below the $1,000,000 department_head approval threshold, submitting them rapidly in succession to meet the tight delivery deadline.
review_evidence: Multiple purchase requests created by the same requester (USER-REQ-001) for the same vendor (VENDOR-014) and project (PRJ-2026-Q2-017) within a short timeframe.
Each request amount below the $1,000,000 department_head approval threshold but cumulative amounts exceed $1,000,000.
Approval levels recorded showing only manager-level approvals on these smaller requests.
Timestamp evidence showing rapid succession of requests to meet the needed_by date 2026-06-30.
